plan: Step 1: Install Playwright as a dev dependency — `npm install -D @playwright/test` and run `npx playwright install --with-deps chromium` (only Chromium needed for smoke tests).

Step 2: Create `playwright.config.ts` at project root. Configure: baseURL pointing to Vite dev server (webServer directive with `npm run dev`), testDir `tests/e2e`, single Chromium project, screenshot on failure.
Step 3: Add `test:e2e` script to package.json — `"test:e2e": "playwright test"`.

Step 4: Create `tests/us-017-snapshots.test.ts` (Vitest) — snapshot regression tests for the three generators: (a) `generateSite()` full pipeline with 5 fixed seeds covering all eras, snapshotting `{ era.id, archetype.id, region.id, theme.id, metadata, abandonmentDetails }`, (b) `generateMetadata()` with 3 seeds × 3 era/archetype combos (verifying existing US-008 snapshots are complemented), (c) `generateName()` with 3 seeds × 3 archetypes, (d) `selectTheme()` with each era verifying theme id mapping. All use `toMatchSnapshot()` so any generator change triggers a diff.

Step 5: Create `tests/e2e/` directory and `tests/e2e/us-017-smoke.spec.ts` (Playwright) with four smoke tests: (a) **First load without seed** — navigate to `/`, verify `#generated-site` has content (`.generated-page` exists), URL gets a `?seed=` param, no console errors. (b) **First load with seed** — navigate to `/?seed=test-abc123`, verify page renders, URL still contains `seed=test-abc123`, content is deterministic (snapshot the page title/header text). (c) **Refresh button flow** — load page, read initial seed from URL, click `.refresh-button`, verify seed in URL changed, `#generated-site` still has content, new content differs from initial. (d) **Share-link reproduction** — load page with `?seed=fixed-seed`, capture header text and hero text, navigate away then back to same URL, verify header and hero text match exactly.
Step 6: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` — fix any TypeScript or lint errors in the new test files.
Step 7: Run `npm run test` — verify all Vitest tests pass including new snapshot tests. Update snapshots if needed with `--update`.
Step 8: Run `npm run test:e2e` — verify all Playwright smoke tests pass. Debug any failures related to Vite dev server startup or DOM selectors.
Step 9: Run full CI suite (`npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test && npm run test:e2e`) to confirm all scripts pass end-to-end.
testing_recommendations: Run `npm run test -- tests/us-017-snapshots.test.ts` in isolation first to verify snapshot tests generate and pass.
Run `npx playwright test --headed` to visually confirm smoke tests interact with the correct elements.
After snapshots are established, intentionally modify a generator output (e.g., change a name fragment) and verify the snapshot test fails — then revert.
Test the share-link reproduction smoke test with multiple fixed seeds to confirm determinism holds across page navigations.
Verify no console errors appear during any Playwright test by asserting on `page.on('console')` for error-level messages.
Run the full CI suite on a clean install (`rm -rf node_modules && npm ci && npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test && npm run test:e2e`) to simulate CI.
risks: Playwright installation may require system-level browser dependencies on CI — ensure `npx playwright install --with-deps chromium` runs in CI pipeline.
Vite dev server startup timing in Playwright's webServer config — may need a `reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI` flag and appropriate timeout.

Snapshot tests are brittle by design — any intentional generator change (new name fragments, metadata templates) will require `--update` to regenerate snapshots. Document this in CLAUDE.md or test file comments.

The share smoke test relies on `navigator.clipboard` or `navigator.share` which may not be available in headless Chromium — may need to grant clipboard permissions in Playwright context or test the fallback path.

Existing US-007/008/009 already have snapshot sections — the new US-017 snapshots cover the integrated `generateSite()` pipeline, but developers must understand which snapshot file to update when generators change.
